error_en: Treating it like a thin steak: it needs control and a little more time.
Overshooting the temperature: it climbs quickly at the end.
Cooking it only over direct heat: it burns outside before the center is ready.
Moving it too much and losing the crust.
Burning the fat cap before rendering it.
Slicing it in the wrong direction.
Overcooking it: it gets tough quickly.
Not slicing against the grain.
Overcooking it: it loses juice and turns greasy or dry.
Slicing it immediately: it needs rest to stay juicy.
Cooking them fast over direct heat: they turn tough and burnt.
Starting at maximum heat: it burns before becoming tender.
Overshooting the temperature: it is lean and dries very fast.
Skipping the rest: the chop loses juice when sliced.
Browning hard before cooking through: the skin burns while the center stays underdone.
Keeping them over constant flame: they burn before staying juicy.
Holding it past 74C for too long: it dries quickly.
Cooking it only direct: it burns outside before the center is ready.
Moving it too much: the skin sticks and tears.
Cooking without drying the skin: it loses crust and sticks.
Using excessive heat the whole time: it dries before browning well.
Turning it too often: it breaks and loses juices.
Burning the outside before the inside softens.
notes_en: Use oil salt and controlled direct heat. Pull when tender and browned.
tips_en: Base time: 25 min|Cut evenly|Oil before grilling
